visualizerParams.title: Millionth Lexicographic Permutation
visualizerParams.description: Find the millionth lexicographic permutation of the digits 0 through 9.
visualizerParams.ghostCode:
from itertools import permutations


def solution() -> str:
    """Returns the millionth lexicographic permutation of the digits 0-9."""
    # itertools.permutations generates permutations in lexicographical order
    # if the input is sorted.
    digits = "0123456789"
    # We use islice to efficiently skip to the millionth element
    from itertools import islice
    target_index = 999999  # 1,000,000th element (0-indexed)
    
    mill_perm = next(islice(permutations(digits), target_index, None))
    return "".join(mill_perm)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    print(solution())
visualizerParams.finalCode:
from itertools import permutations


def solution() -> str:
    """Returns the millionth lexicographic permutation of the digits 0-9."""
    # itertools.permutations generates permutations in lexicographical order
    # if the input is sorted.
    digits = "0123456789"
    # We use islice to efficiently skip to the millionth element
    from itertools import islice
    target_index = 999999  # 1,000,000th element (0-indexed)
    
    mill_perm = next(islice(permutations(digits), target_index, None))
    return "".join(mill_perm)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    print(solution())
